items.q: What is the dramatic function of a soliloquy?
items.correct: It reveals a character's inner thoughts directly to the audience while the character is alone on stage — a convention granting access to private psychology.
items.misconception: A soliloquy is any long speech a character gives.
items.random_wrong: A soliloquy is when characters argue with each other.
items.why: Students confuse soliloquy (alone, inner thoughts) with monologue (long speech, possibly to others). The key feature is solitude and direct access to thought — losing this loses the convention's force.

items.correct: A fatal error or flaw in the tragic hero that leads to their downfall — sometimes a character defect, sometimes a mistake in judgement.
items.misconception: Hamartia is the tragic hero's evil personality.
items.random_wrong: Hamartia is the moment the tragic hero dies.
items.why: Tragic heroes are not villains. Hamartia is often a noble quality taken too far (Othello's love, Macbeth's ambition) or a misjudgement — not simple wickedness.

items.q: What is the difference between a theme and a symbol?
items.correct: A theme is an abstract idea or concern the text explores (e.g. mortality); a symbol is a concrete image that stands for something else (e.g. a skull).
items.misconception: Themes are just symbols repeated through the book.
items.random_wrong: A theme is the title of the book.
items.why: Students treat theme and symbol as the same thing. Themes are ideas, symbols are images. Skulls (symbol) can develop the theme (mortality), but they are not the same level of abstraction.

items.q: Whose 1565 Italian novella collection provided the source for Othello?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cinthio's Hecatommithi. “Boccaccio's Decameron” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In approximately which year was Othello first performed?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1603-04. “1599-1600” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In which year is Othello generally believed to have been composed?
items.why: The real figure is 1603. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
